items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8 and will be rescheduled for a later date. Residents expecting updates on this development will need to wait for a new hearing date to be announced.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project Moves Forward
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to a consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7. This infrastructure project addresses traffic and rail safety at a key corridor in San José.

items.headline: City Awards Contract for Local Streets Pavement Replacement
items.summary: San José awarded a contract on April 7 for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project. This street maintenance work will repair and replace deteriorating pavement across the city's local street network.

items.headline: Cupertino Updates Active Transportation Plan with New Scoring
items.summary: The city presented Phase 2 of its Active Transportation Plan on April 7, including revised criteria for evaluating biking and pedestrian projects. This plan will shape future investments in how residents can get around without cars.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract to move it forward. Details about the specific project, location, and cost were not provided in the agenda summary.

items.headline: Milpitas updates housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, which guides federal funding for housing and community development projects. This could affect future affordable housing initiatives and neighborhood improvements in Milpitas.

items.headline: Senior housing complex with 65 units approved in East San José
items.summary: Trillium senior apartments at 678 E St John St represents a significant new housing option for older residents, with nearly $8.8M investment in 65 units. This addresses growing demand for age-appropriate housing in the region.

items.headline: City Council Approves Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy
items.summary: San José City Council approved a new Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy on April 1, which will determine how and where temporary housing for unhoused residents can be established across the city. This policy change could affect where navigation centers, safe parking programs, and other interim housing sites appear in various neighborhoods.
